nodes.label: Digital Twin + AI before CMMS (Build Order Decision)
nodes.rationale: Decided to prioritize product differentiators (Digital Twin + AI) over maintenance workflows; CMMS deferred to Phase 4 to validate platform with customers first
nodes.norm_label: digital twin + ai before cmms (build order decision)

nodes.label: GitHub Branch Protection Rules (main and dev)
nodes.norm_label: github branch protection rules (main and dev)

nodes.label: Architecture Evolution Principle
nodes.rationale: MVP simplicity -> pilot reliability -> enterprise scalability. Avoid heavy infra before real need; keep boundaries clean for upgrades.

nodes.label: Modular Monolith Pattern
nodes.rationale: Do not split into microservices too early. A modular monolith API gateway is acceptable until there is clear pressure.

nodes.label: Event Streaming Options
nodes.rationale: Do not use Kafka only because it sounds enterprise. Choose it only if sensor volume, replay, and data pipelines justify it.

nodes.label: Observability Pillars
nodes.rationale: Observability is required because Digital Twin FM will run multiple services, ingest live data, support realtime dashboards, and may influence facility operations.

nodes.label: Structured JSON Logging
nodes.rationale: All services log structured JSON in production. Do not log secrets, full JWTs, or large raw sensor payloads.

nodes.label: PWA-First Mobile Strategy
nodes.rationale: Faster development, shared code with Next.js, easier deployment, good enough for many technician workflows, avoids maintaining iOS/Android too early.

nodes.label: QR Asset Lookup Flow
nodes.rationale: Technician scans QR on equipment -> opens /assets/:assetId -> sees asset status, open work orders, recent alerts. QR codes encode stable asset URL/ID, not temporary data.

nodes.label: Staged Offline Rollout
nodes.rationale: Full offline mode is complex. Introduce gradually: online UI, cache assigned WOs, then offline notes/sync. Risks: conflicting updates, stale safety info, duplicate submissions.

nodes.label: KPI Definitions (MTTA/MTTR/Health Score/SLA)
nodes.rationale: Define KPIs precisely before implementation: Building Health Score, MTTA, MTTR, SLA compliance.

nodes.label: Data Quality in Analytics
nodes.rationale: Analytics must account for missing sensor data, offline sensors, estimated values, changed thresholds, changed asset mappings, model/version changes. Reports should show data quality warnings.

nodes.label: Full-Product Security Principles
nodes.rationale: Authenticate every user/device. Authorize every business action server-side. Least privilege. Scope by org/site/building. Never expose secrets. Audit critical actions. Prefer secure defaults.

nodes.label: RBAC + Resource Scoping Model
nodes.rationale: Start with RBAC, then add resource scoping (org/site/building) and attribute checks (ownership/assignment).

nodes.label: AI Security Boundaries
nodes.rationale: AI must not bypass permissions. Retrieval filters by user org/site/building access. No secrets in prompts. Critical action requires confirmation.

nodes.label: Full-Product Guiding Principle
nodes.rationale: Do not overbuild the MVP. However, every MVP decision should keep a clean migration path toward the full product. MVP: prove value with simulated/seeded data. Full product: run safely against real facilities.

nodes.label: Software-First & Demo Impact Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Build the system against a simulated sensor stream. Hardware abstraction with dual-mode ingestion. Digital Twin + AI prioritized. Maintenance deferred post-Expo.

nodes.label: MVP Execution Priority
nodes.rationale: Dashboard -> Digital Twin Viewer -> Realtime Sensors -> Alerts -> AI Copilot -> Maintenance/CMMS later. Core promise is AI-powered digital twin facility insight, not generic work-order system.

nodes.label: Scalability Rules (Start Simple)
nodes.rationale: Docker Compose before Kubernetes. Redis before Kafka. REST before GraphQL. Simple anomaly rules before complex ML. GLB/GLTF or simplified 3D before IFC/BIM. Upgrade only when real requirements demand it.

nodes.label: REST-First API Style
nodes.rationale: MVP uses REST first. GraphQL not in MVP. Easier for team, clear endpoint ownership, works for CRUD-heavy facility software, easier to test and document.

nodes.label: Roadmap Shift Rationale
nodes.rationale: Original roadmap was technically strong but put too much weight on maintenance/work orders before the digital twin and AI experience. Most FM products already have maintenance; differentiate with digital twin + AI first.

nodes.label: Why Full Maintenance is Deferred
nodes.rationale: Full maintenance is effectively a mini-CMMS: workflow state, permissions, assignments, activity logs, notifications, technician UX. Valuable but should not block the Expo demo.

nodes.label: Microservices-Ready Monorepo
nodes.rationale: Microservices-ready monorepo architecture allows independent scaling of domains (Asset Management, Real-Time Monitoring, AI Assistant) while maintaining unified developer experience.

nodes.label: Future-Proofing Strategy
nodes.rationale: API First, IaC ready for K8s/Helm, event-driven via Redis/PubSub to transition to Kafka if volume demands, LLM-agnostic abstraction over OpenAI/Anthropic/local models.

nodes.label: JWT + RBAC Auth Model
nodes.rationale: MVP uses JWT authentication with role-based access control. Roles: admin, facility_manager, technician, viewer.

nodes.label: MVP Permissions Matrix
nodes.rationale: All /api/* business endpoints require authentication. Viewers read-only. Technicians limited. Facility managers manage assets/alerts. Admins manage users and all resources.

nodes.label: Branch Strategy (main/dev/feature/fix)
nodes.rationale: main=production protected, dev=integration auto-deploys to staging, feature/* off dev, fix/* off dev, chore/*, docs/*. Never commit directly to main or dev.

nodes.label: Conventional Commits Convention
nodes.rationale: Format: <type>(<scope>): <short description>. Types: feat, fix, chore, docs, test, refactor, perf, ci. Subject <72 chars, no period.

nodes.label: Domain Ownership Table
nodes.rationale: Per-domain owners (Akshay/Sahil/Sumanth/Sudhanva). Shared packages (db, ui, types) need extra care and 2+ approvals.

nodes.label: TypeScript & Code Standards
nodes.rationale: No any. Strict mode. Zod DTOs. Server Components by default. React Query for server state, Zustand for UI. One controller per domain, DTO validation before service layer. No raw SQL (Drizzle).

nodes.label: Work-Order Lifecycle (deferred)
nodes.rationale: Lifecycle: open -> assigned -> in_progress -> completed. Also: open -> cancelled; assigned -> blocked -> in_progress. Statuses: open, assigned, in_progress, blocked, completed, cancelled. Belongs to Phase 7/post-MVP.

nodes.label: Health Endpoints (/health, /ready)
nodes.rationale: /health checks process alive. /ready checks dependencies: database, Redis, AI provider, connectors.

nodes.label: Dashboard vs Report Distinction
nodes.rationale: Dashboard: live/near-real-time, interactive, operational. Report: historical, exportable, scheduled, shareable, evidence-oriented.

nodes.label: Dual-Mode Ingestion (simulate vs live)
nodes.rationale: Hardware abstraction with dual-mode interface (Simulator vs Live IoT Gateway) to swap in real sensors later without changing backend code.

nodes.label: Real-time Sensor Flow
nodes.rationale: sensor/simulator -> ingestion-service HTTP -> validate/normalize -> insert sensor_readings hypertable -> publish Redis event -> api-gateway subscribes -> WebSocket broadcast -> web updates live UI.

nodes.label: Alert Flow
nodes.rationale: sensor reading -> threshold evaluation in ingestion-service or api-gateway domain -> create/update alert in PostgreSQL -> publish alert event -> web receives alert update over WebSocket.

nodes.label: AI Copilot Flow
nodes.rationale: web asks question -> api-gateway validates/authenticates -> ai-service receives scoped context request -> AI returns answer/citations/suggestions -> api-gateway returns response to web.

nodes.label: Infisical Secrets Management
nodes.rationale: Infisical is the secrets-management direction for the platform. Local fallback for development. Documented in DATABASE_AND_SECRETS_STRATEGY.md.

nodes.label: Deployment Rollout Stages
nodes.rationale: Stage 1: Docker Compose local. Stage 2: Pilot (single VM, managed PG/Redis, Caddy/Nginx, automated backups). Stage 3: Enterprise (K8s/Helm only if needed, private cloud/on-prem, secret manager, blue/green).

nodes.label: Facility Data Hierarchy
nodes.rationale: organization -> site -> building -> floor -> zone/room -> asset -> sensor. Adds organizations, sites, zones, devices, connectors, audit_logs, attachments, reports, model_versions to MVP schema.

nodes.label: 5 Critical Security Findings
nodes.rationale: Critical: SHA-256 passwords; timing-attack compare; CORS wildcard+credentials; no JWT verification; deterministic dev JWT secret. Each is a ship-blocker.
nodes.author: Hermes

nodes.label: 10 High Security Findings
nodes.rationale: Missing security middleware, public endpoints, unauthenticated ingestion, error-leaking UI, public dashboards.
nodes.author: Hermes

nodes.label: 12 Medium Security Findings
nodes.rationale: Infrastructure hardening, type drift, broken infra claims, hygiene.
nodes.author: Hermes

nodes.label: Design Tokens (status.ok/warn/crit)
nodes.rationale: Tokens: Primary, Secondary, Backgrounds, Surface, status.ok/warn/crit, Typography, Spacing. Shared across monorepo via packages/ui/tokens.ts.

nodes.label: 9-Step Scaffolding Protocol
nodes.rationale: Install Prerequisites; Scaffold Monorepo; Create GitHub Repo; Configure Tooling; Wire up CI; Docker Compose; Create ZenTao Tasks; Define Design Tokens; First Commit+PR.

nodes.label: Incremental Domain Folder Scaffolding
nodes.rationale: Optional placeholders for maintenance/ directories with README explaining deferred Phase 7 scope. Don't build full work-order screens for Expo unless core digital twin + AI demo is already complete.
